question: Q2
title: Parent active-child chronology is a canonical-save-owned field that graph refresh preserves
summary: The 026 parent keeps `last_active_child_id` pointing to `004-code-graph`; graph derivation and merge explicitly preserve that pointer, while `generate-context.ts` updates only the direct phase parent after a canonical save.

question: Q2
title: Draft 027 placeholders are marked complete by the status heuristic
summary: 027 phases 003 through 006 are draft in spec tables but have graph metadata status `complete` because placeholder implementation summaries exist without checklists or YAML frontmatter statuses.

question: Q2
title: 026 resource-map drift is an explicitly stale snapshot
summary: The resource map warns that it is a pre-wave-4 snapshot and should not be used for navigation, so this drift is stale active documentation rather than direct evidence of a live generator failure.

question: Q2
title: 027 description metadata retains stale renumbering
summary: The 008 learning-feedback-reducers description still says phase 009 and carries `specId` 007; the generator can derive these fields from the current folder name, so the stale file likely was not regenerated after rename.

question: Q2
title: 026 changelog index and top rollup disagree
summary: The 026 changelog index reports 128 leaf changelogs and 14 rollups for track 000, while the track 000 top rollup lists one child phase, showing catalog rollup drift after later expansion.

question: Q2
title: A reference-remediation packet itself carries conflicting completion metadata
summary: The README/reference accuracy packet describes 142 fixes across 61 files while its continuity still says 30 percent and the metadata table still says In Progress.
